sprint: Selection Action Strip — Real Repaint Bug + Context Menu Becomes the One Real Editor
task: a real, user-reported screenshot showed the just-shipped strip working correctly but ALSO showed the same editing control duplicated in the right sidebar panel below it. Asked how the two should relate; user's answer: "i like context menu better." Mid-turn the user escalated to something more urgent: "i think its something with repaint. the changes seems to be happening but not showing up on center pane", then: "whre the options would be comes later. i shared screenshot to show you that the change is not reflecting on center pane" -- deprioritizing the duplicate-editor decision in favour of a real, previously-unreproduced canvas-repaint bug. Root cause, found via an interleaved-typing reproduction (a single .fill() test had shown the redraw pipeline working correctly, pointing the investigation at the strip's own code instead): js/selectionActionStrip.js's refresh() -- called from PageRuntime.notify()'s dispatch list, which fires on essentially every mutation anywhere in the app -- had one line, if(_editOpen) _openEditPanel(), meant to 'keep an already-open popup in sync.' But _openEditPanel() tears the popup down and rebuilds it from scratch (a fresh <textarea> with no focus), so a Story Author typing into the popup could have it silently swapped out from under their cursor by something completely unrelated, discarding every keystroke typed after that -- directly violating this module's own already-stated 'never rebuild mid-edit' rule. Confirmed via a git stash A/B test with genuinely interleaved keystrokes: pre-fix registered only the first character; fixed code registered the full string with the same DOM node persisting throughout. Fix: removed the offending line -- a genuinely new selection already closes/resets the popup via the key-changed branch above it, so nothing was left to 'keep in sync' by rebuilding here. Design decision implemented in the same pass: js/contextPanel.js's _renderWorldObjectDisclosure now shows only the status banner/hint (pointing at the strip's own popup) when SelectionActionStrip is loaded, instead of also mounting a second, redundant live control bound to the same value. A resulting test regression (in the test, not the app): commitments_test.js used an unscoped querySelector that silently matched an unrelated Theme Designer element once the right panel's own copy was removed -- fixed by clicking the strip's real Edit button first and scoping the query to #selectionActionEditPanel. Verified via a new 8-assertion Playwright suite (repaint_fix_verify.js) built around interleaved typing. Full regression across selection_action_strip_test.js (15/15), selection_strip_pencil_fix.js (10/10), text_resize_grid_test.js (12/12), canvas_click_visual_repro.js (6/6), paperbg_hittest_repro.js (3/3), sync_charm_test.js, and the corrected commitments_test.js all pass unchanged, plus both goldenBuild.js suites. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
